document: South Africa National Cybersecurity Policy Framework (NCPF) 2015

sections.heading: 1 . Introduction
sections.summary: Introduces South Africa's National Cybersecurity Policy Framework (NCPF) as part of government's outcome-based priorities to enhance safety through cybersecurity measures, recognizing cyber threats' personal, national, and international dimensions.
sections.source_quote: 1.1 A number of strategic interventions and tactical interventions have been successfully implemented over the past few years and other interventions are in the process of being implemented within the Justice, Crime Prevention and Security (JCPS) Cluster in the fight against crime with the objective of making South Africa Safe.

sections.heading: have the potential to impact on our national security and economy.
sections.summary: Highlights South Africa's vulnerability to cybercrime, noting phishing and e-crime incidents targeting the country, with the banking sector being especially vulnerable.
sections.source_quote: 2.4 Statistics in 2011 indicate that South Africa was in the top three countries that are targeted for phishing purposes, the other countries are the USA and the UK.

sections.heading: freedom of expression.
sections.summary: Outlines the purpose of the NCPF to create a secure cyberspace that protects critical information infrastructure while strengthening shared human values and understanding of cybersecurity.
sections.source_quote: 3.1 The purpose of the NCPF is to create a secure, dependable, reliable and trustworthy cyber environment that facilitates the protection of critical information infrastructure whilst strengthening shared human values and understanding of Cybersecurity in support of national security imperatives and the economy.

sections.heading: Key Objectives of the NCPF
sections.summary: Sets strategic priorities for the NCPF, including centralizing cybersecurity coordination, fostering public-private cooperation, promoting international collaboration, and developing cybersecurity skills.
sections.source_quote: 4.1 The NCPF articulates the overall aim and objectives of the South African Government and sets out strategic priorities that will be pursued to achieve these objectives.

sections.heading: Capacity to Respond to Cybersecurity Imperatives
sections.summary: Establishes a Cybersecurity Response Committee to oversee implementation, coordinate cybersecurity activities, and ensure protection of national critical information infrastructure.
sections.source_quote: 5.2 A dedicated JCPS Committee will be established within the JCPS Cluster to coordinate Cybersecurity activities, drive the implementation of the NCPF and manage the implementation of Output 8.

sections.summary: Establishes Cybersecurity Hub within DOC to coordinate consultation between public sector, private sector, and civil society on cybersecurity matters.
sections.authority: Department of Telecommunications and Postal Services (DOC)

requirements.text: Coordinate general Cybersecurity activities, in consultation with JCPS CRC as well as including identifying stakeholders and developing public-private relationships and collaborating with any sector CSIRTs that may be established
Disseminate relevant information to other sector CSIRTs, vendors, technology experts on Cybersecurity developments
Provide best practice guidance on ICT security for Government, business and civil society
Initiate Cybersecurity awareness campaigns
Promote compliance with standards, procedures and policy developed by the JCPS Cybersecurity Response Committee regarding Cybersecurity matters with a bearing on national security
Encourage and facilitate the development of appropriate additional sector CSIRTs

sections.source_quote: The Cybersecurity Hub will be operated within the DOC in accordance with national security guidelines and standards issued by the JCPS Cybersecurity Response Committee.

sections.heading: Verification of Information Security Products and Systems
sections.summary: Promotes development of National Information Security Verification Framework for assessing and certifying products/systems impacting national security.

requirements.text: Facilitating effective partnerships between the Republic of South Africa and countries with established capacity to perform information security assessments and certifications

Facilitating effective partnerships between the Government of South Africa, the private sector, academic and research institutions to ensure capacity for information security assessments and certifications within the borders of the Republic
Developing National regulations for verification of products and systems with applications in Information Security
Facilitating effective partnerships among government institutions to ensure solutions are implemented in accordance with certification conditions and legislation
Establishing a body that will centrally coordinate the required national verification functions

sections.source_quote: The NCPF therefore promotes the facilitation by the JCPS Cybersecurity Response Committee and the National Cybersecurity Hub of the development of a National Information Security Verification Framework

sections.heading: NCII Protection
sections.summary: Requires identification and protection of National Critical Information Infrastructure (NCII) through regulations and business-government partnerships.

requirements.text: Developing National Critical Information Infrastructure regulations, relating, inter alia, to: Information Classification and Information Security Policy and Procedures
Third Party Access to NCII
Access to and authentication on NCII
Storage and archiving of critical databases
Incident management and business continuity
Physical and technical protection of all NCII

sections.source_quote: The NCPF therefore seeks to ensure that appropriate steps are taken to ascertain that all National Critical Information Infrastructure (NCII) are identified and properly protected from a variety of threats.

sections.heading: Cryptography
sections.summary: Recognizes need to review existing cryptography legislation and develop an integrated regulatory framework.

requirements.text: Review the existing legislation and regulations thereof
Develop an integrated regulatory framework for Cryptography for the country

sections.source_quote: The NCPF recognises that there is a need to: Review the existing legislation and regulations thereof; and Develop an integrated regulatory framework for Cryptography for the country.

sections.summary: Promotes development of integrated National E-identity and PKI strategy for secure e-government services.

requirements.text: Promote the development of an integrated National E-identity and PKI strategy

sections.source_quote: The NCPF seeks to address the fragmented approach by promoting the development of an integrated National E-identity and PKI strategy.

sections.heading: 1 1 . Promote and Strengthen Local and International Cooperation
sections.summary: Promotes public-private-civil sector collaboration to enhance cybersecurity through joint knowledge, threat understanding, and trusted forums.

requirements.text: Promotes public-private-civil sector collaboration to enhance cybersecurity through joint knowledge, threat understanding, and trusted forums.

sections.source_quote: 11.2.2 The NCPF thus promotes the establishment of collaboration with local stakeholders, with a focus on the following aspects: (a) Inclusion of the industry and creating an enabling environment for a successful partnership; (b) Encouraging private sector groups to address common security interests and collaborate with government including encouraging cooperation among groups from interdependent industries; (c) Bringing private sector and government together in trusted forums; and (d) Creating a common understanding of the threats and vulnerabilities that the country faces and the responses required.
